title: Hippolytia leucophylla
taxonomy: Hippolytia leucophylla is classified in the family Lamiaceae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as mint family. This family includes a wide variety of species, many of which are used in cooking and herbal medicine. The genus Hippolytia is a part of the Lamiaceae family and is primarily found in the mountainous regions of Asia. Hippolytia leucophylla is a relatively lesser-known species within this genus, but it is still of interest to botanists and plant enthusiasts. Further research may be necessary to determine the full classification and taxonomy of Hippolytia leucophylla.
